title: euonymus macropterus
intro: Euonymus macropterus, also known as the winged spindle or winged Euonymus, is a deciduous shrub native to eastern Asia, characterized by its distinctive winged samaras and red, orange, or yellow berries. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with its bark and leaves exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. These medicinal properties have been associated with various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving rheumatic pain, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In traditional herbal preparations, Euonymus macropterus is often combined with other herbs to create teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
